title: Fine-tune a VLA Robotics Policy on Solo Hub
description: From a recorded LeRobot dataset on HuggingFace to a trained VLA policy in your Hub org. Requires Plus or Pro plan. Can be started directly after solo_cli_guide dataset recording.

check_plan.instruction: VLA fine-tuning requires Plus or Pro plan. What plan are you on? (Check Settings → Billing)

check_plan_in_billing.instruction: Go to Settings → Billing. Your current plan is shown at the top of this page.

on_failure.recovery: If you can't access billing, your role may not be Admin — ask your org Admin to check

upgrade_plan_required.instruction: VLA fine-tuning is locked on the Basic plan. Go to Pricing in the Hub navbar and upgrade to Plus ($20/mo) or Pro ($100/mo). After upgrading, return here.

on_failure.recovery: Confirm upgrade in Settings → Billing before proceeding. If it hasn't reflected after 2 minutes, refresh the page.

check_credits.instruction: Check your credit balance in the Hub navbar. VLA jobs run longer than LLM — do you have enough credits? (A 50k-step job on H100 can take several hours at ~12 credits/hr)

buy_more_credits.instruction: Go to Settings → Billing → Buy Credits. For a full 50k-step VLA run, the Gold pack ($50, 275 credits) gives you comfortable headroom.

on_failure.recovery: You need Admin role on your org. Or email may not be verified — check both.

check_dataset.instruction: Do you have a LeRobot-compatible dataset on HuggingFace? You need: the repo ID (owner/dataset-name) and the exact camera count in your dataset.

use_vla_example.instruction:
Solo provides example VLA datasets:
- GetSoloTech/Candy-Pick — pick task, 2 cameras
- GetSoloTech/Juice-Serving — pour task, 2 cameras
- GetSoloTech/FoodStack — stacking task, 2 cameras

For your first VLA fine-tune, use GetSoloTech/Candy-Pick with camera_feeds = 2.

create_dataset_guidance.instruction: To create a VLA dataset from scratch, use solo robo --record via the CLI. This creates a LeRobot-compatible dataset automatically. After recording, push it to HuggingFace. Then come back and start this tutorial from verify_camera_count.

verify_camera_count.instruction: Open your dataset on HuggingFace and check meta/info.json. Look in the 'features' section and count keys that start with 'observation.images' (e.g. observation.images.top, observation.images.wrist). This number MUST match the Camera Feeds value you set in the wizard.
verify_camera_count.validation_prompt: How many camera keys are in your dataset's meta/info.json features section?

on_failure.recovery: Navigate to your HuggingFace dataset page → Files tab → meta/info.json. If this file is missing, the dataset may not be LeRobot-compatible.

wizard_step1_type.instruction: Open the fine-tuning wizard in Solo Hub. Click 'VLA' (camera icon). If you see a 'locked' overlay, your plan upgrade hasn't reflected yet — refresh the page.

on_failure.recovery: Check Settings → Billing to confirm your plan is Plus/Pro. Refresh the page and try again.

wizard_step1_model.instruction: Which VLA policy do you want to fine-tune? Choose based on your task type and hardware budget.

guidance.act: Best for bimanual / fine manipulation. A100 40GB is fine.
guidance.smolvla: Compact and efficient. Great for limited data and edge deployment. A100 40GB is fine.
guidance.pi05: Open-world generalization. Recommend A100 80GB or H100 80GB.
guidance.groot_n15: Strong language following, humanoid tasks. Recommend A100 80GB or H100 80GB.

select_act.instruction: Select ACT. Good for manipulation tasks. A100 40GB is sufficient for most camera counts.

on_failure.recovery: Ensure VLA type is selected first — model cards only appear after VLA is chosen

select_smolvla.instruction: Select SmolVLA. Compact, efficient, good on limited data. A100 40GB is sufficient.

on_failure.recovery: Ensure VLA type is selected first

select_pi05.instruction: Select Pi0.5. Open-world generalization. Use A100 80GB or H100 80GB for best results.

on_failure.recovery: Ensure VLA type is selected first

select_groot.instruction: Select GR00T N1.5. Strong language conditioning and humanoid support. Use A100 80GB or H100 80GB.

on_failure.recovery: Ensure VLA type is selected first

wizard_step2_gpu_small_model.instruction: Select A100 40GB or H100 80GB. ACT and SmolVLA work well on 40GB VRAM for up to 4 cameras. H100 finishes faster and burns fewer total credits.

on_failure.recovery: Do not select A10 24GB — Insufficient VRAM badge means the job will fail at provisioning

wizard_step2_gpu_large_model.instruction: Select A100 80GB or H100 80GB. Pi0.5 and GR00T N1.5 strongly recommend 80GB VRAM. Using 40GB with 3+ cameras will likely fail at provisioning.

on_failure.recovery: If you must use A100 40GB, limit camera_feeds to 1 or 2 and use a smaller batch size

wizard_step3_params.instruction:
Set VLA parameters:
- Max Steps: 50000 (recommended for a full run; use 10000–20000 for a quick test)
- Batch Size: 16 (recommended)
- Camera Feeds: MUST exactly match your dataset's actual camera count (from meta/info.json)
wizard_step3_params.validation_prompt: Confirm: the Camera Feeds value matches the number of camera keys you counted in meta/info.json?

on_failure.recovery: Go back to verify_camera_count — open your HuggingFace dataset's meta/info.json and count the observation.images.* keys before proceeding

wizard_step4_data.instruction: Enter your HuggingFace dataset repo ID (format: owner/dataset-name). For example datasets: GetSoloTech/Candy-Pick. Camera Feeds must already be set correctly in Step 3.
wizard_step4_data.validation_prompt: Is the repo ID in owner/dataset-name format? Does Camera Feeds in Step 3 match your dataset?

on_failure.recovery: VLA requires LeRobot-format datasets. If using CLI-recorded data: ensure it was recorded with solo robo --record and pushed to HuggingFace.

wizard_step5_integrations.instruction: Do you want to configure optional integrations (HuggingFace Hub push or W&B tracking)?

configure_integrations.instruction: For HF Hub push: toggle on, enter token (hf_xxx format, write access) and repo name (username/model-name). For W&B: toggle on and enter your W&B API token. You can skip either or both.

on_failure.recovery: Verify HF token has write scope at huggingface.co/settings/tokens. Ensure W&B project exists before launching.

wizard_step6_launch.instruction: Review the cost estimate. VLA jobs run longer than LLM — a 50k-step job can take several hours. Confirm you have enough credits (check max_runtime shown). Click 'Start Training'.
wizard_step6_launch.validation_prompt: After clicking Start Training: do you see a results card with a Job ID and status = QUEUED?

error_map.Insufficient credits: VLA jobs consume more credits than LLM. Add more via Settings → Billing → Buy Credits. Or reduce Max Steps for a test run.
error_map.Job already exists: Change the Output Model Name — it must be unique within your org
error_map.VLA locked: Plan downgraded or not applied yet — check Settings → Billing and refresh

monitor_vla_job.instruction: Click the model link in the results card → open the Diagnostics tab. VLA jobs spend most time in the Training stage. Pipeline: QUEUED → Provisioning → Initializing → Training → Uploading → Completed. If it fails at Provisioning or Initializing, most likely cause is camera count mismatch or dataset format issue.
monitor_vla_job.validation_prompt: Is the job progressing through pipeline stages in the Diagnostics tab? What stage is it in now?

on_failure.recovery: Check camera count matches dataset meta/info.json. Check dataset is LeRobot-compatible. Re-launch with corrected values.

tutorial_complete.instruction: VLA fine-tuning complete! Your trained policy is in your Solo Hub org. Download the model weights from the Files tab, then use with solo robo --inference on the CLI to run the policy on your robot.
